task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL application is open and '1UBQ' is visible in the object list on the right-hand panel.

task_completion.description: Enable solvent-accessible area calculations and compute SASA
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL command line or history shows commands like 'set dot_solvent, 1' and 'get_area' being executed for the lysine residues.

task_completion.description: Display the overall protein as a grey or white surface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main PyMOL viewer shows the protein structure rendered as a solid surface colored in grey or white.

task_completion.description: Display the 7 lysine residues as distinctly colored spheres
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the PyMOL viewer, specific residues (the 7 lysines) are rendered as spheres in a distinct color (e.g., red) that protrude from the grey/white protein surface.

task_completion.description: Generate a text report with the 7 lysines and their SASA values sorted in descending order
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displays the contents of 'ubiquitin_lysine_report.txt', showing exactly 7 lysine residues (K6, K11, K27, K29, K33, K48, K63) with their SASA values, ordered from highest to lowest.

task_completion.description: Save a high-resolution, ray-traced image of the visualization
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL console shows a 'ray' command followed by a 'png' command saving to '~/PyMOL_Data/images/ubq_lysines.png', or a file manager/image viewer shows the final rendered image at that path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used PyMOL to create the visualization
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the PyMOL GUI being actively manipulated, with the 3D viewer matching the final saved image.

integrity.description: Verify the SASA values are genuinely computed floats
integrity.visual_evidence: The SASA values in the report should be realistic decimal numbers (between 0.0 and 300.0) extracted from PyMOL, not generic placeholders or integers.

integrity.description: Verify the image was actually ray-traced before saving
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'ray' command in the PyMOL command history or a ray-tracing progress bar, rather than just a standard viewport capture.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target PDB: 1UBQ. Target Lysines: 6, 11, 27, 29, 33, 48, 63. Valid SASA values must be between 0.0 and 300.0 squared Angstroms. The report must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/ubiquitin_lysine_report.txt and sorted in descending order. The visualization must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/ubq_lysines.png.
